name: Chinese Noodle Salad w/ Roasted Eggplant
recipeIngredient: 4 ounces snow peas (strings removed, cut into narrow strips)
recipeInstructions:
1. Begin by making the marinade. Combine all ingredients except noodles in a bowl and stir until sugar is dissolved.
2. Bring water to boil for noodles. Gently pull apart strands, add to boiling water without salt, cook briefly until done but not soft, then rinse cold water.
3. Shake colander vigorously to remove excess water and transfer noodles to a bowl. Stir marinade, pour half over noodles, toss with hands to coat.
4. Set remaining marinade aside. If not using immediately, cover noodles and refrigerate.
5. Preheat oven to 400°F. Pierce eggplants several times and bake until soft and skins shriveled (about 20 minutes).
6. Turn eggplants after 10 minutes for even cooking. Remove from oven, peel skin, shred into 1/4-inch strips.
7. Add ginger and garlic to marinade, then eggplant strips. Mix well and set aside.
8. Bring a quart of water with a teaspoon of salt to boil, blanch snow peas until bright green, remove and rinse in cool water.
9. Cut snow peas into long narrow strips and set aside.
10. Cook mung bean sprouts for 30 seconds in boiling water, drain, lay on kitchen towel to dry.
11. Roast sesame seeds until lightly colored and toasted, then set aside.
12. If noodles have been refrigerated, allow them to come to room temperature; toss with eggplant strips and half the sesame seeds.
13. Mound salad on a platter, distribute carrots, snow peas, and mung bean sprouts over noodles, garnish with remaining sesame seeds and cilantro leaves.
14. Serve layered or on individual plates. Guests can toss noodles and vegetables together to mix colors, textures, and flavors.
Variations: Use roasted peanuts or cashew nuts instead of sesame seeds. Include blanched asparagus tips in spring and thinly sliced radishes among garnishes.
